subject_line: US market entry partnership — INBRAIN Neuroelectronics
notes: $50M Series B (June 2024). BCI for neural mapping. Spain-based. CEO explicit US clinical expansion signals.
email_body:
Subject: US market entry partnership — INBRAIN Neuroelectronics

Hi Carola,

INBRAIN just closed a $50M Series B — that's a serious signal you're heading toward US commercialization. Most EU neurotech companies underestimate how different the US clinical and reimbursement landscape is, especially for BCI devices.

I'm a US-native MBA based in Madrid. I help neurotech companies like yours navigate US market entry — from regulatory strategy to distribution channel setup. I also run an AI-powered outbound system that identifies exactly which US hospital systems, research centers, and payers are actively looking for what you're building.

The $5K question: what's your US market entry timeline, and are you actively evaluating distribution partners?

Happy to show you exactly how I'd approach INBRAIN's US push — Happy to jump on a 20-min call this week.

Best,
Jordan Hays
US Market Entry Consultant | MBA | Madrid-based

notes: $48.8M Series B. AI-based BCI for stroke recovery. US FDA breakthrough designation. Israeli, targeting US hospital systems.
us_signals: FDA breakthrough designation, US hospital partnerships
email_body:
Subject: US market entry partnership — BrainQ

Hi Yotam,

BrainQ's FDA breakthrough designation for stroke recovery BCI — that's a US market opener. Most Israeli neurotech companies don't realize how much the US reimbursement landscape changes the commercial calculus once you're inside a hospital system.

I'm a US-native MBA based in Madrid who helps neurotech companies like yours establish US distribution. My AI-powered prospecting system identifies which US hospital networks are actively building stroke and neurorehab programs — so we can target the right partners from day one.

Would a 20-min call this week make sense to map out your US entry options?

Best,
Jordan Hays

notes: $35.9M raised. tDCS for epilepsy and brain research. Spanish company with strong US research hospital relationships (Harvard, UCLA).
us_signals: Partnerships with Harvard, UCLA research programs
email_body:
Subject: US market entry partnership — Neuroelectrics

Hi Ana,

Neuroelectrics has established relationships with Harvard and UCLA research programs — the question is whether you're ready to move from research partnerships to commercial US distribution.

I'm a US-native MBA based in Madrid with a specific focus on neurotech US market entry. I know the difference between selling to research institutions vs. commercial healthcare networks — and the path is very different for each.

Happy to map out what a US commercial expansion looks like for Neuroelectrics specifically. 20 minutes?

Best,
Jordan Hays

notes: FDA cleared Dec 2025 — first at-home tDCS for depression. Sweden-based. Series status unclear. Significant US market entry momentum.
us_signals: FDA cleared for at-home use, US market entry imminent
email_body:
Subject: US distribution partnership — Flow Neuroscience

Hi Daniel,

FDA clearance for at-home tDCS for depression — that's a real milestone. The US mental health market is wide open for non-pharma, non-invasive device interventions, and you've cleared the regulatory hurdle.

The challenge now isn't the product — it's the distribution. US mental health provider networks, telehealth platforms, and insurance reimbursement paths are a completely different game from Europe.

I'm a US-native MBA based in Madrid who helps neurotech companies establish US commercial channels. I have specific relationships with US mental health providers and telehealth platforms actively looking for FDA-cleared devices like yours.

Open to a 20-min call this week to map the opportunity?

Best,
Jordan Hays

subject_line: US Veterans market entry — GrayMatters Health
notes: FDA cleared. tBSG device for PTSD/depression. Israeli. Targeting US Veterans Affairs (VA) system — high-value but complex government channel.
us_signals: FDA cleared, targeting VA and military health systems
email_body:
Subject: US Veterans market entry — GrayMatters Health

Hi Oded,

GrayMatters Health cleared by FDA for tBSG treatment of PTSD and depression — and you're targeting the VA system. That's a high-value, high-complexity channel that most companies underestimate.

The US Veterans Affairs procurement process is a distinct discipline — different stakeholders, different timelines, different compliance requirements than commercial healthcare.

I help neurotech companies navigate exactly these government channels. Happy to map out what a VA market entry strategy looks like for GrayMatters. 20 minutes?

Best,
Jordan Hays
